title: Army Heritage & Education Center News
raw_text:
Army Heritage Center Foundation:
250 years of Army Soldier Stories
The Army Heritage Center Foundation shares the stories of U.S. Army soldiers from all eras, fostering understanding and building meaningful connections between the military and civilian communities. We accomplish this through public outreach activities like community-building, resource-sharing, education, and philanthropic investments in the preservation of Army history. As a private, nonprofit, non-Federal entity, AHCF is not affiliated with the Department of Defense and has no governmental status.
About the Foundation
About Us - Video
Our Primary Beneficiary: The U.S. Army Heritage and Education Center
The U.S. Army Heritage and Education Center collects, preserves, and presents materials related to the evolution and application of landpower, as well as the experiences of American Soldiers, to build a foundation for strategic thought and to connect the public to the United States Army.
Learn More
Upcoming Events
Give
to Honor the American Soldier
The Army Heritage Center Foundation depends on the generosity of our donors to fulfill our mission. Donate today and join us in honoring the heritage of the American Soldier. Together, we can cultivate a world that invests in the preservation of soldier storiesвАУand learns from them.
Donate Now
Subscribe for Updates
Subscribe to stay informed about Foundation news, events and initiatives.

Last

The content describes the organization's mission, solicits donations, and invites newsletter subscriptions.

title: Food Processing Residuals (FPR)
raw_text:
Food Processing Residuals (FPR)
FPRs are waste left over from food manufacturing, which can include vegetable peelings, raw meat scraps and liquid, like cleaning wastewater that includes blood, fat, hair and feathers.
In Pennsylvania, FPR can legally be applied to farmland as an inexpensive way to increase the amount of organic matter in soil.
However, residents are concerned about this particular product being used due to possible environmental contamination, noxious odors, and improper handling and application.
Rep. Barb Gleim has been at the forefront of this issue and was appointed a member of a newly formed statewide workgroup to come up with a plan to address the challenges FPR presents in Pennsylvania.
The group is working to make sure these waste byproducts coming into Pennsylvania from outside the state are regulated and safe for the environment and our farmlands, are transported and stored appropriately, and are not a nuisance to residents surrounding our agricultural lands.
See below for news articles, updates and more information about food processing residuals in the Commonwealth.

Barb Gleim was appointed to a newly formed statewide workgroup focused on regulating food processing residuals (FPR) вАФ waste byproducts from food manufacturing that are legally applied to Pennsylvania farmland. The workgroup is developing recommendations to address environmental, odor, and safety concerns associated with FPR use. Previous legislation related to this issue stalled in the state Senate as of January 2025.

title: House Democrats Continue Disregard for Safety of PennsylvaniaвАЩs Female Athletes
raw_text:
House Democrats Continue Disregard for Safety of PennsylvaniaвАЩs Female Athletes
June 17, 2026
HARRISBURG вАУ State Rep. Barb Gleim (R-Cumberland) condemned TuesdayвАЩs House Health Committee partisan vote to yet again re-refer
House Bill 158
,
Senate Bill 9
and
House Bill 1849
to the House Judiciary Committee. вАЬThis is nothing more than the latest attempt to avoid a public vote on protecting girlsвАЩ and womenвАЩs sports,вАЭ said Gleim.
Consideration of House Bill 1849 has now been delayed for more than three months since its introduction, Senate Bill 9 has been awaiting House action for more than a year, and House Bill 158 has been stalled in committee for more than 16 months.
вАЬYesterdayвАЩs actions are about delay and not policy,вАЬ Gleim added. вАЬPennsylvaniaвАЩs girls, parents, coaches and athletes deserve the opportunity to have their voices heard. Instead, House Democrats chose procedural gamesmanship over transparency and accountability.вАЭ
The re-referral is particularly troubling because both the House Health and Judiciary committees will have seen these bills twice, calling into question the sincerity of their previous statements.
вАЬFemale athletes should not be forced to sacrifice opportunities that countless women have fought to secure,вАЭ Gleim commented.  вАЬI will continue working to advance these bills and ensure the voices of PennsylvaniaвАЩs female athletes are not ignored.вАЭ
Representative Barb Gleim
199th Legislative District
Pennsylvania House of Representatives

raw_text:
Gleim, Owlett: U.S. Supreme Court Backs Basic Biology in Groundbreaking Ruling
June 30, 2026
HARRISBURG - The U.S. Supreme Court today echoed the countryвАЩs overwhelming public position that men should not be permitted to compete alongside girls in sports designated for women only. According to state Reps. Barb Gleim (R-Cumberland) and Clint Owlett (R-Tioga/Bradford), the 6-3 decision and corresponding court opinion also offered an extremely important distinction вАУ that biological sex, not вАЬgender identity,вАЭ should be the determining and distinguishing factor for separating sports.
The groundbreaking ruling confirms that states have the authority and responsibility to protect female athletes. While Gov. Josh Shapiro and House Democrats constantly dodge and duck the issue, the Supreme Court is confirming that PennsylvaniaвАЩs governing athletic body, the PIAA (Pennsylvania Interscholastic Athletic Association), and local school directors should be compelled to step in and restore sanity.
вАЬWeвАЩre calling on the PIAA and school boards across the Commonwealth, who have not already acted to protect their girl athletes, to enact policies now,вАЭ said Owlett. вАЬWe are thankful for the nearly two dozen school districts here in Pennsylvania and the 27 states nationwide who have taken seriously protection of our girl athletes.вАЭ
Protecting girlsвАЩ sports has long been fought for via House Republican legislation. GleimвАЩs original House Bill 972 of 2021, legislation that would have ensured fairness for girl athletes, passed both chambers of the General Assembly in bipartisan fashion, only to be vetoed by Democrat Gov. Tom Wolf. Several similar bills which have been introduced this session -
Senate Bill 9
, which passed with bipartisan support;
House Bill 1849
and
Senate Bill 1293
- have been summarily sidestepped by House Democrats to prevent a vote.
вАЬThis is a momentous decision for girls and women who have fought long and hard for fairness in sports,вАЭ said Gleim. вАЬBecause of the DemocratsвАЩ inaction, the ball is now in the PIAAвАЩs court. ItвАЩs finally time to reinstitute fairness, protect our girl athletes, and affirm biological reality.вАЭ
Representative Barb Gleim
199th Legislative District
Pennsylvania House of Representatives

title: Gleim Announces $1 Million RACP Funding Recipient in Middlesex Township
raw_text:
Gleim Announces $1 Million RACP Funding Recipient in Middlesex Township
July 17, 2026
HARRISBURG
вАУ Rep. Barb Gleim (R-Cumberland) announced today that Carlisle Community Improvements has been awarded a $1 million Redevelopment Assistance Capital Program (RACP) grant to support the renovation and expansion of the Carlisle Country Club clubhouse.
The funding will help advance the project by covering both design and construction costs associated with the clubhouse improvements. Grant funds will be used for concrete, carpentry, drywall, HVAC, plumbing, sprinkler, finishes, electrical work, and windows and doors, helping bring the project to completion.
вАЬThis investment is great news for our community,вАЭ Gleim said. вАЬThe Carlisle Country Club has long been an important part of Cumberland County, and these improvements will enhance the facility, while strengthening the areaвАЩs ability to host events and attract visitors. I am pleased to see state funding used to move this project forward.вАЭ
RACP provides financial assistance for the acquisition and construction of regional, economic, recreational, and historical improvement projects that generate lasting benefits for Pennsylvania communities.
Representative Barb Gleim
199th Legislative District
Pennsylvania House of Representatives
